A '''shell flute''' was a shell drilled with a pattern of holes which was used by members of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Water/Legends|aquatic]] [[Mairan/Legends|Mairan]] [[Species/Legends|species]] to communicate in their [[Mairan language|native language]]. To use the [[Flute/Legends|flute]], a Mairan individual would place the drilled side of the shell against its blowhole and play the [[Music/Legends|music]]-like notes of the Mairan [[Language/Legends|language]]. In [[23 ABY/Legends|23 ABY]], when [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]], the [[Hapan]]-[[Dathomir/Legends|Dathomiri]] [[Prince/Legends|princess]], was staying on [[Reef Fortress Island]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hapes/Legends|Hapes]], she used the [[Translator droid/Legends|translator droid]] [[M-TD]] to speak with a [[Unidentified Mairan ambassador|Mairan ambassador]] who was using a shell flute.<ref name="Lightsabers" />

==Behind the scenes==
The shell flute first appeared in ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'', a [[1998]] young reader's novel by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Rebecca Moesta]] as the fourth entry in the ''[[Star Wars: Young Jedi Knights]]'' series.<ref name="Lightsabers" />

==Appearances==
*''[[Young Jedi Knights: Lightsabers]]'' {{1st}}
